iClick Interactive Asia Group Limited will hold an EGM on January 3, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. (Hong Kong time). NASDAQ: ICLK.
news.summary: Announced on December 19, 2024, iClick Interactive Asia Group Limited is planning to convene an extraordinary general meeting for shareholders on January 3, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. (Hong Kong time). The meeting is significant for discussing crucial matters regarding the company's operations. As a publicly listed entity on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol ICLK, iClick aims to engage shareholders closely in the decision-making process. The EGM is scheduled to address key issues that will impact the future direction of the company. Shareholders have been encouraged to participate actively in this meeting, which will have a bearing on the strategic initiatives of iClick Interactive Asia Group Limited.

Wharton's Jeremy Siegel says stock sell-off 'healthy'; Fed lowers rates to 4.25-4.5%, less cuts expected for 2025. Market reacts with declines as investors were overly optimistic. Fed may reduce rates once or twice next year, or not at all due to inflation concerns.
news.summary: Jeremy Siegel, a professor emeritus at the University of Pennsylvania’s Wharton School, characterized the recent drop in the stock market as a "healthy" response to the Federal Reserve's cautious stance on future interest rate cuts. The Federal Reserve's decision to decrease interest rates by 0.25%, setting the target range at 4.25% to 4.5%, was accompanied by a revised outlook indicating fewer rate reductions in 2025 than previously anticipated. This announcement caused declines in all three major Wall Street indices as investors were expecting more aggressive rate cuts. According to CNBC, Siegel stated that the market had been excessively optimistic, leading to the sell-off. He suggested that the Fed might only lower rates once or twice next year, or even potentially not at all due to concerns about inflation. The Fed's updated forecasts anticipate that the core personal consumption expenditures price index will... (for full story, check Benzinga.com).

news.headline: Hithium introduced specialized sodium-ion battery ∞Cell N162Ah for utility-scale energy storage at Hithium Eco-Day.
news.summary: On December 12th, 2024, in Beijing, China, at the second Hithium Eco-Day event, Hithium introduced the ∞Cell N162Ah, the inaugural sodium-ion battery intended for utility-scale energy storage. This innovative battery is optimized for broad temperature variations and high-rate performance.

news.headline: Rio Tinto appoints Georgie Bezette as new Chief People Officer in place of James Martin, starting Jan 1, 2025.
news.summary: Rio Tinto has announced the appointment of Georgie Bezette as the new Chief People Officer, succeeding James Martin who will retire at the end of the year. Bezette, currently serving as the Chief Operating Officer, People, joined the company in 2008. She is tasked with overseeing the transformation of the People function to align with Rio Tinto's cultural change initiative. Bezette will assume her new position on January 1, 2025, stationed in London. Rio Tinto's Chief Executive, Jakob Stausholm, expressed confidence in Bezette's appointment, emphasizing her role in advancing the company's organizational development.

news.headline: Telix Pharmaceuticals completed installation of 2 cyclotrons at TMS in Brussels South, Belgium.
news.summary: Telix Pharmaceuticals Limited has recently finished installing two brand new cyclotrons at Telix Manufacturing Solutions in Brussels South, Belgium. This development comes as a significant step for the company, listed on ASX and Nasdaq under TLX, as they expand their production capabilities. The installation of these cyclotrons marks a major investment, aiming to enhance the manufacturing process of medical products. This move reflects Telix's commitment to advancing their technological infrastructure.

news.headline: Solana DApp revenue hits $365M in Nov 2024, with Pump.fun earning $106M despite controversy, led by memecoins.
news.summary: Pump.fun, a platform that has encountered controversy recently, reported an impressive revenue of $106 million from its DApp in November.

Atlassian appoints Christian Smith, Splunk's Chief Revenue Officer, to its Board of Directors effective January 1, 2025. Smith is expected to aid in accelerating enterprise focus.
news.summary: Atlassian Corporation (NASDAQ: TEAM), a prominent provider of team collaboration and productivity software, has revealed the appointment of Christian Smith to its Board of Directors, starting from January 1, 2025. Christian, currently serving as the Chief Revenue Officer at Splunk, a renowned cybersecurity and observability company, will bring valuable expertise to Atlassian's board. This move comes as Atlassian aims to enhance its enterprise focus, with Christian's sales acumen expected to play a significant role in this strategy.

Energy Dome and ENGIE sign storage offtake agreement for CO2 Battery in Ottana, Italy; to be operational in Q1 2025.
news.summary: In Milan, Energy Dome, a leading long-duration energy storage (LDES) technology company, has signed an offtake agreement with ENGIE, a renowned low-carbon energy and services provider. This agreement is for the first full-scale CO2 Battery developed by Energy Dome in Ottana, Sardinia, Italy. The CO2 Battery is set to be commissioned in the first quarter of 2025, making it one of the few operational projects in the field of cleantech technologies. This collaboration marks a significant step in the global transition towards commercial deployment of sustainable energy solutions.

news.headline: Bata appoints Miguel Esquide as Chief Design Officer to boost innovation and creativity in footwear fashion globally.
news.summary: Switzerland's city of Lausanne witnessed an important announcement on December 19, 2024, when Bata, a prominent player in the footwear and fashion industry, revealed the appointment of Miguel Esquide as the Chief Design Officer of Bata. This strategic hiring underlines Bata's dedication to driving innovation and fostering creativity in its continuous evolution...

news.summary: In the final quarter of 2024, notable events in the financial sector have emerged, with heightened tensions in the Middle East leading to a spike in oil prices and China implementing robust stimulus actions to revitalize its economy. These developments have captured global attention and impacted various markets.

news.headline: Dow drops over 1,100 points for the 10th consecutive session as Powell takes hawkish stance, Greed index in 'Fear' zone.
news.summary: The CNN Money Fear and Greed index signaled a significant drop in market sentiment, shifting to the “Fear” zone. U.S. stocks closed lower on Wednesday, with the Dow Jones index plummeting for the 10th consecutive session, experiencing a decline of over 1,100 points throughout the day. Following the December Fed meeting, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell took on a more hawkish tone during his press conference. Despite an expected 0.25% decrease in interest rates to a range of 4.25%-4.5%, the updated economic forecasts indicate only two possible rate cuts in 2025. This is down from the four rate reductions forecasted in September and less than the three expected by markets prior to the meeting.

news.headline: CarMax (NYSE: KMX) to release Q3 results on 12/19. Analysts predict EPS of 61 cents (up from 52 cents) and revenue of $6.05 billion (down from $6.15 billion). Shares fell 2.1% to $81.42.
news.summary: CarMax, Inc. (NYSE:KMX) is set to unveil its third-quarter financial results on Thursday, Dec. 19. Analysts are predicting earnings of 61 cents per share, up from 52 cents a year ago. The Richmond, Virginia-based company is expecting quarterly revenue to be $6.05 billion, a slight decrease from $6.15 billion in the previous year. CarMax previously reported earnings per share of 85 cents on Sept. 26, which fell short of the estimated 86 cents. Following this news, CarMax's shares dropped 2.1% to close at $81.42 on Wednesday. For more details and analyst ratings, readers can visit Benzinga.com.

TargED Biopharmaceuticals initiates Phase 1 trial for TGD001, a novel thrombolytic for ischemic stroke and TTP, promising faster, safer treatment for clotting disorders. Phase 1 aims for safety study, with potential future development in AIS and iTTP patients.
news.summary: A new drug, TGD001, is currently in development to provide individuals with clotting disorders a treatment that is faster, safer, and more effective compared to the current standard of care. This drug aims to save lives and minimize disability among patients. After completing the Phase 1 safety study, TargED plans to progress with the development of TGD001 for patients with Acute Ischemic Stroke (AIS) and Immune Thrombocytopenic Purpura (iTTP).

news.headline: AI's impact on G7 tourism could be significant, says OECD policy paper. It may improve sustainability, visitor experiences, and address risks.
news.summary: A policy paper from the OECD emphasizes the significant impact of AI on the tourism sector of the G7 countries. AI has the potential to transform G7 tourism, improving sustainability efforts and enhancing visitor experiences. The paper also addresses the importance of managing risks associated with AI implementation in the tourism industry.

news.headline: Sivers Semiconductors and Ayar Labs to expand partnership to enable high volume manufacturing of optical I/O solutions for AI infrastructure.
news.summary: The enhanced cooperation will center on certifying products and preparing for production of Sivers’ precise laser arrays for large-scale implementation of optical I/O solutions in AI Datacenters. The initiative will take place in KISTA, Sweden, with plans set for completion by December 19, 2024. Sivers Semiconductors AB (STO) will be spearheading these efforts.

news.headline: Implantica expands RefluxStop™ in Germany; added key University Hospital with more in pipeline. Acid reflux has 1 billion sufferers.
news.summary: In Vaduz, Liechtenstein on December 19, 2024, Implantica AG (publ.) revealed groundbreaking technology aimed at treating acid reflux with their innovative device, RefluxStop™. This medtech company is leading the way in incorporating advanced technology into the human body. The treatment of acid reflux is a vital area as it affects a staggering 1 billion individuals worldwide. The company's announcement marks a significant advancement in addressing this widespread health issue.

news.headline: Xi Jinping praises Macao's success with "one country, two systems" during visit.
news.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Macao on Wednesday afternoon, emphasizing the success of the "one country, two systems" policy with Macao characteristics. The approach has garnered global recognition for its vitality and charm. This statement was made on Dec. 19, 2024, without specifying the source of this information.

news.summary: The recently released report highlights a significant increase in unemployment rates across the country, with the national average now standing at 7.2%. This marks a sharp rise from last year's rate of 5.5%. The report also indicates a substantial decline in job creation, with only 150,000 new jobs added last month compared to the previous year's average of 250,000 new jobs per month. The manufacturing sector has been hit particularly hard, experiencing a 10% decrease in employment. Experts attribute these alarming trends to ongoing economic challenges and the impact of the global pandemic. Despite government efforts to stimulate the economy, the job market continues to face uncertainties, leaving many concerned about the future.

news.headline: Hillstone Networks reports successful 2024, predicts future growth by 2025. Leading cybersecurity provider.
news.summary: In December 2024, based in Santa Clara, California, Hillstone Networks, a prominent cybersecurity solutions provider, celebrates its successes throughout the year. 2024 has witnessed significant growth and expansion for the company, with a strong emphasis on innovation and advancing defenses against cyber threats.

news.headline: Huawei celebrates 5 years of AppGallery Editors' Choice Awards in 2024, recognizing top mobile apps and games.
news.summary: Huawei is celebrating the fifth anniversary of the AppGallery Editors' Choice Awards 2024 in London. This event recognizes groundbreaking apps and games for their innovation and creativity. The selection was made by Huawei Europe's editor teams, highlighting exceptional applications.

news.headline: Ottimo Pharma secures $140M+ in Series A funding from OrbiMed, Avoro Capital, and Samsara BioCapital. Leadership team expanded.
news.summary: A recent round of funding, predominantly spearheaded by OrbiMed, Avoro Capital, and Samsara BioCapital, along with continued support from original investor Medicxi, has been successfully secured. This financial boost will fortify the company's position in the industry. Noteworthy additions to its leadership include Rick Anderson, welcomed as the Senior Vice President of Finance, Spencer Fisk as the Chief Technical and Quality Officer, amongst others.

news.headline: Henkel to reveal future of laundry & dish care at CES 2025, with smart dosing technology innovations.—150 characters.
news.summary: Henkel, a prominent global player in consumer and industrial sectors, is set to unveil cutting-edge smart dosing technology innovations in DÜSSELDORF, Germany on Dec. 19, 2024. This move is part of the company's commitment to revolutionize the cleanliness sector. The showcase aims to outline Henkel's blueprint for the next generation of laundry products, highlighting its dedication to pioneering advancements in this field. The event will emphasize the company's forward-thinking approach and reinforce its position as an industry trailblazer.
